title: Authored-gap reason prose (domain_alignment *_not_addressed[].reason) is not free-text-scanned
description: TASK-040 added authored deferred-gap entries {label, reason} to domain_alignment.*_not_addressed. The label is catalogue-validated, but the reason prose is not run through the free-text audit (the catalogue-survivor / fabrication scan) the way the targeted rows' rationale is, and _iter_prose_fields in check_draft does not include the reason field. A reason could name a fabricated entity or off-register text without detection. Low-impact (reason is a brief structured field), but a real asymmetry vs the targeted rationale path.

evidence.reference: FREETEXT_SELECTORS_BY_SPEC scans targeted rationale prose; the not_addressed reason field is not a selector

impact: A fabricated/off-register reason on a deferred-gap entry escapes the free-text/catalogue audit. Low blast radius (brief structured field) but an enforcement asymmetry.
proposed_resolution: CLOSED by TASK-050 / VER-045 / commit 3597879: _select_a3 (planner/_freetext_audit.py) now extracts the deferred-gap `reason` alongside `rationale`, routing domain_alignment.*_not_addressed[].reason through the SAME audit_prefill_flags catalogue-survivor scan as targeted rationale (field-presence based; mutual exclusivity enforced by the parser builders). Production fix; full gate green; audit verified end-to-end through the real parser (fabricated reason -> flag; clean -> none). SCOPE NOTE: the whole-draft check_draft._iter_prose_fields not walking domain_alignment is a separate pre-existing matter, not this parse-time catalogue-survivor asymmetry. [Original plan: add the *_not_addressed[].reason field to the free-text audit selectors so deferred-gap reasons are scanned like targeted rationales.]
